title: Safe resume boundary (SOP §10)

public_api.FileSnapshotVerifier: class — hashlib-based file state verifier (verify + snapshot helpers)
public_api.ResumeCoordinator: class — end-to-end resume decision entry (decide_resume method)
public_api.build_pending_operations: function — reconstruct pending ops list from SessionEvent log
public_api.OPERATION_TERMINAL_EVENTS: dict — terminal event types to state values

design_decisions.title: FileSnapshotVerifier as a class, not a bare callable
design_decisions.rationale: evaluate_resume_safety accepts a Callable[[dict], bool] for testability (synthetic verifiers in tests). FileSnapshotVerifier is the production implementation with a stateful hash_algorithm config and a companion snapshot() helper for recording pre-mutation state. Keeping them in one class lets callers reuse one instance for both snapshot and verify.

design_decisions.title: build_pending_operations pairs events by operation_id, last-write-wins
design_decisions.rationale: An operation may have multiple started events (retries) or multiple terminal events (committed then failed — unusual but possible). Last-write-wins matches the idempotency ledger semantics. Order is stable: operations returned in order of first started event, so tests can assert on ordering.

design_decisions.title: ResumeCoordinator composes evaluate_resume_safety rather than duplicating its rules
design_decisions.rationale: P0-C's evaluate_resume_safety is the source of truth for the core decision (registry hash, pending ops, file snapshots). The coordinator only gathers inputs (latest_checkpoint, event log reconstruction, active worker detection) and delegates. This keeps the decision logic in one place and lets tests for evaluate_resume_safety stay focused.

design_decisions.title: Active Worker detection is detection-only (no recovery)
design_decisions.rationale: SOP §10.3 explicitly defers MultiAgent active Worker recovery to a later version. The coordinator detects status in {active, running, started} and blocks resume with recovery_required, but never tries to terminate or reconcile the Worker. This is the conservative safe default.

design_decisions.title: Schema version gate before any other check
design_decisions.rationale: A Checkpoint with schema_version=99 means the serialized format may have changed in ways the coordinator cannot read. Checking schema version BEFORE evaluate_resume_safety avoids passing a misread Checkpoint to the decision logic (which might then make a wrong decision based on misinterpreted fields).

design_decisions.title: FileSnapshotVerifier.verify returns False on I/O errors, never raises
design_decisions.rationale: evaluate_resume_safety treats False as a mismatch (safe default: block resume). A file that cannot be read (permission denied, vanished mid-read) has unknown state — treating it as False is safer than raising, because raising would force the caller to add try/except around every verify call. The snapshot() helper, by contrast, records absence rather than crashing.

design_decisions.title: existence_required=False records ABSENCE (not just non-existence)
design_decisions.rationale: A snapshot with existence_required=False asserts 'this file should NOT exist when resume runs'. Used for cleanup operations that delete files — if the file appears on resume, an external process created it, and resume MUST stop. This is a positive assertion about absence, not a default for missing files.

test_matrix_coverage.R-01_step_boundary_resume: test_r01_clean_checkpoint_returns_ok — clean Checkpoint + matching registry → ok
test_matrix_coverage.R-04_external_file_change: test_r04_external_file_change_returns_recovery_required — file content modified after snapshot → file_conflicts populated + recovery_required
test_matrix_coverage.R-05_corrupt_checkpoint: DEFERRED to v0.04.1 (E-DEBT-4) — SOP §12 R-05 defines 'Corrupt checkpoint — state_hash 失败'. state_hash recomputation requires a helper that knows the original state at checkpoint time. Current test_no_checkpoint_returns_recovery_required covers the related 'no Checkpoint exists' case (SOP §10.1 condition 1 failure), which is a distinct scenario.
test_matrix_coverage.R-06_active_worker_marker: test_r06_active_worker_returns_recovery_required — TaskState status='active' → recovery_required (detection only, no recovery)

10.1_auto_allow_resume.condition_3_no_pending_mutation: build_pending_operations finds no non-terminal ops (delegated to evaluate_resume_safety Rule 3)
10.1_auto_allow_resume.condition_4_taskstate_revision_consisten: NOT enforced by coordinator — caller responsibility; v0.04 does not auto-reconcile (deferred to v0.04.1)
10.1_auto_allow_resume.condition_7_budget_state_valid: Trivially satisfied — v0.04 Checkpoints carry budget_state={} until Phase F wires real budget tracking

sop_10_boundary_compliance.10.2_must_stop_recovery_required: Negation of 10.1 plus RECOVERY_REQUIRED on any file hash mismatch, pending operation in non-terminal state, or active Worker marker. Condition 7 (lease 状态无法确认) is NOT detected — see E-DEBT-5.

10.3_v0.04_does_not_promise.no_auto_replay_mutating_tool: Coordinator never calls a mutating tool — only computes decision
10.3_v0.04_does_not_promise.no_active_worker_recovery: Detection only — never attempts to terminate or reconcile Workers

full_suite_regression: 344 passed, 1 skipped (was 320 passed, 1 skipped before Phase E; +24 Phase E tests, no regression)

non_blocking_debts.description: TaskState revision consistency check (SOP §10.1 condition 4) is not enforced by ResumeCoordinator
non_blocking_debts.impact: A caller that bumped TaskState revision after the Checkpoint would not be detected. v0.04 assumes the caller committed TaskState BEFORE the Checkpoint (correct ordering per SOP §5.3).
non_blocking_debts.deferred_to: v0.04.1 — needs a revision-comparison helper that reads Checkpoint.task_state_revision and compares to current TaskState revisions

non_blocking_debts.description: Budget state validation (SOP §10.1 condition 7) is trivially satisfied because Checkpoints carry budget_state={}
non_blocking_debts.impact: No real budget tracking in v0.04 Checkpoints. A run that exhausted budget mid-step would not be detected at resume time.
non_blocking_debts.deferred_to: Phase F — wire real budget state into Checkpoint.budget_state and add validation in ResumeCoordinator

non_blocking_debts.description: TOCTOU race between FileSnapshotVerifier.verify and resume action
non_blocking_debts.impact: A file could be externally modified between verify() returning True and the runner acting on the decision. v0.04 assumes single-process resume with no concurrent writers.
non_blocking_debts.deferred_to: v0.04.1 — needs file locks or a verify-then-act atomic primitive

non_blocking_debts.description: state_hash validation (R-05 in test matrix mentions 'Corrupt checkpoint — state_hash 失败')
non_blocking_debts.impact: Coordinator does not recompute state_hash from current state and compare. A Checkpoint with a tampered state_hash would not be detected. The current test_no_checkpoint_returns_recovery_required covers 'no Checkpoint exists', which is the more common failure mode but is a distinct scenario from R-05.
non_blocking_debts.deferred_to: v0.04.1 — needs a state_hash recomputation helper that knows the original state at checkpoint time

non_blocking_debts.description: Lease status detection (SOP §10.2 condition 7: 'lease 状态无法确认')
non_blocking_debts.impact: ResumeCoordinator does not check FileLease or other lease states. A run that crashed while holding an active lease would not be detected at resume time. v0.04 does not persist leases (SOP §10.3 defers FileLease recovery), but §10.2 requires DETECTION of uncertain lease state — detection and recovery are distinct.
non_blocking_debts.deferred_to: v0.04.1 — needs FileLease persistence layer and a lease-state inspector integrated into ResumeCoordinator._detect_active_workers or a parallel _detect_uncertain_leases method
